title: Hattie's Indigo Mushroom Adventure
summary: Hattie the Mouse and her friend Izzy the Kiddo set off on an adventure to find rare indigo and violet mushrooms for Izzy’s hat. After discovering Hattie's workshop only carries common colors, they hitch a ride with Who the Owl to the corner of Cornell and Poplar Streets. Guided by Uncle X the Owl, they find their way to the Bistrom Store, a unique shop run by a tall, multicolored bear named Cod. After successfully acquiring the rare mushrooms, Izzy decorates her hat and creates a special card for her Nana before heading home to rest after her long expedition.

highlightQuote.text: And my hammers and my elbow pads and my turtle fingers and my finger rings and the earrings, there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: This story is about Hattie the Mouse's house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, no knocks on the door, okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Everyone was sleeping and there's no knocks on Hattie's door.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Everyone was sleeping and there were no knocks on Hattie's door. And then what happened?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Also no knocks.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie the mouse was peacefully sleeping inside her house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And gradually the sky started to get a little bit lighter and the sun peeked up over the horizon and lit up the clouds just a tiny bit. The sky turned gray, and then a little bit blue, and then a little bit lighter, a little brighter, a little at a time. And that's called the dawn. The beginning of the morning, the end of the nighttime and the beginning of the day. And the birds started to quietly chirp, 'chirp-chirp, chirp-chirp.' And the bugs and the bees started to buzz, 'bzzzz.' And everybody, a little bit at a time, gradually started to wake up all around the forest. But Hattie the mouse was still sleeping in her little cozy bed inside of a hollow tree.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie wake up! Wake up Hattie!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who said that? Me, Izzy the Kiddo said that. Were you visiting Hattie's workshop?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I am.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie said, 'Good morning Izzy the Kiddo. What brings you to my workshop today?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I already have a hat so I don't need one of yours, but I would like my mushroom... and I would like an indigo one... but I would like it if it had one mushroom.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you'd like one mushroom? A green one did you say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I would like an indigo one with a violet one on the top of my hat... a violet one and an indigo one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A violet and an indigo mushroom for the top of your hat. Well, that sounds like a difficult job because red and orange and yellow mushrooms are very common, and I have lots of those.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And do you have indigo ones and violet ones?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, those ones are much rarer, there are many fewer of them in the world, and so they're hard to find. I don't think I have any right now.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can you go out to the store and buy some?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You know, it's something that even the store doesn't have. So we're going to have to go out into the forest and search for them. But...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, but store has them. A different store. A store that only has mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A store that only has mushrooms? I've never been to that store.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's on Cornell and Poplar Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, at Cornell and Poplar Street. Okay, well, I'm glad you know exactly where it is. That will help us get there. But first, before we go on this expedition, I need to have a real breakfast. Have you eaten yet?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I already ate my breakfast.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, I'll just have a little bit of something. And Hattie the mouse rummaged around inside her cabinets and she found a delicious little snack. What did she have?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cantaloupe.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She had a bowl of delicious cantaloupe. Nom, nom, nom. And then Hattie the mouse said, 'Well, I've had my breakfast, now I'll just get on my adventuring clothes.' And she put on her red cloak and her beautiful red pointy hat. And she said, 'Okay Izzy the Kiddo, I'm ready to go.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now I have to put on my... suit... for... hair stuff... my braids by playing a little bit... tickle my toes... rinse my chin with the water... and then put on my driving suit... there... and then I have to put on my shoes... and the hat too... and my coat too... and there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There, you're all ready Izzy, said Hattie. Well, let's go. Now, our quest is to find indigo and violet mushrooms for your hat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, and... get on a Twirler Poplar and Cornell Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Aha! Well, there's no streets around here, so I better find someone to ask who can help us get there. And just as Hattie the mouse said that, Izzy and Hattie heard a sound from high up in a tree nearby and it was 'Who, who, who.' And who was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: An owl!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was, and what was the owl's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Who. Who the owl.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who the owl. And Who the owl flew down and said, 'Who, who, hello!' And Hattie said, 'Oh, I'm so glad you're here. Good morning Who the owl. Do you think you could fly us to Cornell Street?' And what did Who the owl say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Yes, who-who, I'm happy to! Hop on!' And so Izzy the Kiddo and Hattie the mouse hopped onto Who the owl's back. And Who the owl flapped, flapped, flapped high up into the air and flew them straight up above the tallest trees in the forest. And now they were flying through the sky looking down on the forest all around them.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, you can't look in the forest, you're supposed to go into the store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Who the owl said, 'Who-who, why yes of course! I'm finding our way to Cornell Street. I think it's over there.' And she dove down and 'whoosh,' the wind blew over Hattie and Izzy as Who the owl flew them through the air over the trees... until suddenly they saw a big long black road cutting through the forest with a yellow line down the middle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Who the owl said, 'Who-who, I think we're here! It's Cornell Street.' And so Who the owl flew them down to the edge of the road and she said, 'Now be careful in case there's any cars.' And Hattie the mouse hopped off of Who the owl's back. And did Izzy hop off too?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, boing! And then I'm going to put on my driving suit and all my other things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Oh that's a good idea because I'm ready...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And my hammers and my elbow pads and my turtle fingers and my finger rings and the earrings, there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'There, yes, you're prepared,' says Hattie the mouse. 'Now, I'm not sure if we should go this way or that way. Which way do you think Poplar Street is?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Take the left and then take the right.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, okay. We'll take a left first.' And so Hattie the mouse bravely set out along the edge of the road... walk, walk, walk.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then do the right, right.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Okay, I got it,' said Hattie. 'Thanks for the directions.' And so Hattie and Izzy walked bravely down the road. Walk, walk, walk.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Are you on the right street? And now are you on Poplar Street?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'I think we're still on Cornell Street, let's keep going.' And then all of a sudden as they came up over a hill and then down the other side they saw something new that they'd never seen before. What was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A big street owl. It was Uncle X.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Uncle X the owl. And what was he doing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He was shoveling some snow that fell from the sky.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He was shoveling some snow. That was very nice of him, because his driveway happened to be right there, leading away from the street over to the little hollow tree that Uncle X the owl lived in. And what did Uncle X the owl say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I live on this street like you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie said, 'Oh yes, your treehouse is very nice, it's bigger than mine.' And what did Izzy the Kiddo say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Of course, and I live on Cornell Street like you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh yes, I live on Cornell Street and so do you,' said Uncle X the owl. And...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Uncle X, I got this little scrape here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, you have a scrape. I'm sorry to hear that. Remember not to itch it.' And where are you two headed, said Uncle X the owl?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We're headed to a store that only has mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'A store that only has mushrooms? Oh yes, that's right down the street. You're headed just the right direction. Just keep going until you get to Poplar Street and then take a right and you'll find the store. What's the name of that store again?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The store that only has mushrooms.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, what's it called?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Bistrom store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, the Bistrom store. Well, have a very nice day, I have to finish shoveling this snow,' said Uncle X. And so Hattie and Izzy continued along Cornell Street until they got to the corner of Cornell and Poplar Street and then what did they do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They went into the store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They took a right and then they saw the Bistrom store, which is a store that only sells mushrooms, and they opened the door and it made a little ding-a-ling. And the creature that owned the store and sold people the mushrooms said hello. And who was that creature?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A bear.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A bear. Was it a little bear or a medium bear or a big bear?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A tall, tall, tall bear.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A tall bear. And what color bear was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a tan and brown and pink bear.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A tan and brown and pink bear. And what was the bear's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cod.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Cod? And the bear said, 'Hello everyone, my name is Cod. What can I do for you today?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I need indigo mushrooms and violet ones too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Ah, yes. The rare and special indigo and violet mushrooms. Well, you've come to the right place. I happen to have some right here.' And he rummaged around in his baskets which had gigantically large red mushrooms and tiny tiny orange mushrooms and big long green and white striped mushrooms...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want the indigo one and the violet one for my hat.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Yes, yes, yes,' said Cod the Bear. 'I'm just searching about, I'll find them.' And then he reached high up onto the topmost shelf and found two tiny little baskets and he brought them down and he brought them to his face and sniffed them. He said, 'Ah yes, such lovely mushrooms. Here's the indigo and here's the violet.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and put them on my hat. There!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: 'Oh, put them on your hat. What a lovely use for mushrooms.' And so what did Izzy the Kiddo do next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And save some so I can put them on my paper for a card for my Nana. Yeah, because I'm going to make a little card because I'm going to write, 'Nana, I love you.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That sounds like a lovely card. Your Nana must be a wonderful person, said Cod the Bear. 'Well, here are your mushrooms,' and he packed them up in a little baggy, a batch of indigo mushrooms and some violet ones as well.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, and then I put them on my hat... there... and then I took off my driving suit... and then my earrings... and my shoes... all my things... things, things, tick, tick, tick... and then I put them back in the garage and then... I got out of the car and then... I was all tired.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're all tired. And that was the end of the story.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Izzy the Kiddo
characters.description: Once a quiet dreamer sketching from a recovery bed, this brave explorer now leads her companions through Hattie’s workshop and the lightless abyss, guided by a beacon of special light. Clad in a rugged driving suit alongside Bluey and Fincat’s Mouse, she wields heavy hammers and a magic wand with logistical precision inherited from her Nana. From trading plastic bricks for rare mushrooms to orchestrating neighborhood cleanups, she balances specialized gear with a playful spirit. Choosing the spark of a game over the gravity of a traditional quest, she ensures her radiant creativity and guiding light remain her most powerful tools for navigating the unknown.

characters.name: Who the Owl
characters.description: A friendly owl who provides air transportation for Hattie and Izzy.

characters.name: Uncle X the Owl
characters.description: An owl living on Cornell Street who spends his time shoveling snow.

characters.name: Cod the Bear
characters.description: A tall, tan, brown, and pink bear who operates the Bistrom store.

characters.name: Nana
characters.description: The dignified mole matriarch and grandmother to Hattie, Pistis, and Isabella, this pillar of watchful wisdom resides at the end of the train line, tackling life’s problems "one at a time." From engineering mouse-scale Lego structures to the meticulous restoration of dollhouses using patient glue and tiny pencils, her steady paws bridge the gap between architectural feats and delicate artistry. Whether discerning potential as a trolley passenger or welcoming weary travelers into her sanctuary, her quiet authority is so legendary that she graces the cover of Hattie’s book in a rare, peaceful slumber. Even in rest, she remains the heart of their world—a steady warmth anchoring every journey’s end, her sharp eyes softening for Izzy’s mushroom-textured crafts.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: Hattie's Hollow Tree
places.description: Hattie's home in the forest where she sleeps in a cozy bed.

places.name: Cornell Street
places.description: Bisected by a stark yellow line, this celestial artery cuts through a dense forest to chart a path toward the Mookers' planet. A cosmic thoroughfare where the infinite is stitched into the earthly, it anchors local commerce—typified by a towering tree of honey—alongside the residence of Uncle X, the house of the surreal bedroom feast, and Hattie’s home. It remains the vital threshold where the mundane pulse of the woods meets the stars, marking the point where Hattie begins her journey to find medical help.

places.name: Poplar Street
places.description: The cross-street of Cornell where the mushroom-only store is located.

places.name: Uncle X's Hollow Tree
places.description: A treehouse on Cornell Street that is notably larger than Hattie's.

places.name: The Bistrom Store
places.description: A specialized shop on the corner of Cornell and Poplar that exclusively sells mushrooms.
